message:
test(syscall): timer_settime/timer_gettime deleted-handle EINVAL precedence (bd-733fn, bd-md74r, bd-6azoc, bd-qq59x)

Single consolidated test (timer_settime_gettime_on_deleted_handle_are_einval)
creates + deletes a timer, then exercises four precedence variants
against the invalid handle. Each must surface EINVAL before any
other argument-validation error:

  bd-733fn  timer_settime(deleted, ..., NULL)
  bd-md74r  timer_settime(deleted, ..., &old)   (writable old_value)
  bd-6azoc  timer_settime(deleted, NULL, NULL)  (null new_value)
  bd-qq59x  timer_gettime(deleted, NULL)        (null output)

Skips cleanly on kernels/containers where timer_create is absent
(ENOSYS/EPERM/EACCES/ENOTSUP).

Verification (rch + CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc):
  cargo test -p frankenlibc-core --test syscall_veneer_test \
             timer_settime_gettime_on_deleted                  1 passed
  cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --tests -- -D warnings      clean
  cargo fmt --check                                             clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
